methods.description: This method describes the process of sampling and analyzing microcosm incubations to quantify dissolved Fe(II)/Fe(total) and NO3-/NO2- concentrations, as well as extracting and quantifying poorly crystalline Fe(II)/Fe(total) fractions.

steps.description: Two milliliters were sampled from each microcosm at each sampling point under a constant N2 atmosphere in an anoxic glovebox, without opening the bottles, with a sterile anoxic syringe and needle (inner diameter of 0.80 mm).
steps.searchable_summary: Sample 2 mL from each microcosm under anoxic conditions using a sterile syringe.

steps.description: These 2 mL samples were centrifuged for 5 min at 15.4 g.
steps.searchable_summary: Centrifuge samples for 5 minutes at 15.4 g.

steps.description: Part of the supernatant was stabilized in 1 M HCl and used for the spectrophotometric ferrozine assay to quantify dissolved Fe(II)/Fe(total) in μmoles·L−1 which was then recalculated to the absolute amount per microcosm.
steps.searchable_summary: Stabilize supernatant in 1 M HCl and perform ferrozine assay to quantify Fe(II)/Fe(total).

steps.description: Another part was frozen anoxically at −20˚C and preserved for dissolved NO3-/NO2- measurement by a flow injection analysis (FIA) system.
steps.searchable_summary: Freeze supernatant anoxically at −20˚C for NO3-/NO2- analysis.

steps.description: The sediment pellet was further incubated anoxically for 1 h on a horizontal shaker at 150 rpm with 0.5 M HCl in order to extract the poorly crystalline Fe(II)/Fe(total) fraction, which was quantified from the supernatant by the spectrophotometric ferrozine assay.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate sediment pellet with 0.5 M HCl and quantify Fe(II)/Fe(total) using ferrozine assay.

methods.method_name: Sampling Site and Initial Sample Treatment
methods.description: This method outlines the collection and initial processing of sediment and water samples from Lake Constance, including microelectrode analysis, water content determination, and carbon content analysis.

steps.description: Littoral sediment and water samples were taken in February 2011 from Lake Constance, a freshwater lake in southern Germany.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect sediment and water samples from Lake Constance.

steps.description: The samples were transported to the laboratory at 4˚C and the sediment was processed immediately for microelectrode analysis, most probable number (MPN) studies and microcosm incubations.
steps.searchable_summary: Transport samples to lab at 4˚C and prepare for analysis.

steps.description: A high-resolution oxygen microelectrode profile was taken from an intact push-core immediately upon arrival in the laboratory.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform oxygen microelectrode profiling on sediment core.

steps.description: The water content of littoral sediment was determined in triplicate by weighing portions of wet sediment, drying them for 4 days at 95˚C, and subsequently determining the dry weight.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine water content by drying sediment and measuring weight loss.

steps.description: Dried samples were pulverized to fine powder from which the percentage of iron by weight was determined using X-ray fluorescence analysis (XRF).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform XRF analysis to determine iron content in dried sediment.

steps.description: The dissolved organic carbon (DOC) and total inorganic carbon (TIC) content of the pore water were determined from a centrifuged portion of wet sediment.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze DOC and TIC content in pore water.

steps.description: The aqueous ferrous iron concentration was measured from the pore water by the spectrophotometric ferrozine assay.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure ferrous iron concentration using ferrozine assay.
